activities_projects.title: Tribes of India Map Activity
activities_projects.objective: Identify major tribal communities in India
steps: Take a blank political map of India
Mark regions where tribes such as Gonds, Bhils, and Santhals lived
Write one cultural feature of each tribe
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn the geographical distribution of tribal communities

activities_projects.title: Nomadic Life Illustration
activities_projects.objective: Understand the lifestyle of nomadic communities
steps: Study how nomadic groups move from place to place
Draw a scene showing a nomadic family traveling with animals or goods
Label key elements such as tents, animals, and tools
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the daily life of nomadic communities

activities_projects.title: Community Lifestyle Chart
activities_projects.objective: Compare tribal, nomadic, and settled communities
steps: Draw three columns for tribes, nomads, and settled communities
Write key features such as occupation, housing, and lifestyle
Decorate the chart with small illustrations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand differences between community lifestyles

activities_projects.title: Tribal Culture Poster
steps: Choose one tribal group
Create a poster showing their clothing, dance, and festivals
Write a short description about their culture
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students appreciate the cultural richness of tribal societies

activities_projects.title: Nomadic Occupations Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the occupations of nomadic groups
steps: Research occupations such as trading, herding, or craft making
List at least five examples
Explain how mobility helps in their work
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how nomads depend on movement for livelihood

activities_projects.title: Tribal Art Drawing
steps: Look at examples of tribal art such as Warli or Gond art
Create your own drawing inspired by these styles
Write two lines describing the art form
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop awareness of tribal artistic traditions

activities_projects.title: Daily Life Observation Activity
activities_projects.objective: Compare modern life with traditional community life
steps: List activities you do in a typical day
Compare them with the lifestyle of tribal or nomadic groups from the chapter
Write three differences and two similarities
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students relate historical lifestyles with modern life

activities_projects.title: Story Writing: A Day in a Tribal Village
steps: Imagine you are visiting a tribal village
Write a short story describing daily activities
Include elements such as food, work, and festivals
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand tribal lifestyle through imagination and writing
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance to chapter concepts (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Community Vocabulary Puzzle
activities_projects.objective: Reinforce key terms from the chapter
steps: List 10 key terms such as tribe, nomad, herding, clan
Create a crossword or word search puzzle
Exchange puzzles with classmates to solve
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students strengthen vocabulary related to the chapter

activities_projects.title: Mini Quiz on Communities
steps: Prepare 10 simple questions from the chapter
Ask classmates to answer them
Keep score for correct answers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students reinforce knowledge through peer learning
